nodes.name: Web Form Trigger
nodes.notes: Entry point for web form leads. Expects JSON body with: name, email, company, companySize, industry.

nodes.name: Gmail Trigger
nodes.notes: Simulates Gmail shared inbox trigger. In production: replace with Gmail Trigger node watching a label like 'sales-leads'.

nodes.name: WhatsApp Trigger
nodes.notes: Simulates WhatsApp Business webhook. In production: replace with Twilio or WhatsApp Business API node.

nodes.name: Normalize Web Form Lead
nodes.notes: Validates required fields, checks email format, and maps to a standard lead shape all downstream nodes expect.

nodes.name: Normalize Gmail Lead
nodes.notes: Extracts structured data from unstructured email. Infers company from domain when not explicitly provided. Throws if no valid email found.

nodes.name: Normalize WhatsApp Lead
nodes.notes: WhatsApp leads rarely include email. We generate a placeholder email so the lead can still be stored and processed — flagged with emailIsPending:true for follow-up.

nodes.name: Merge All Sources
nodes.notes: Combines all three normalized lead streams into one pipeline. After this point, all leads have the same shape regardless of source.

nodes.name: Check Existing Client List
nodes.notes: Mock client database check. Flags existing clients so sales team isn't wasting time re-pitching current customers. In production: query CRM or Google Sheets.

nodes.name: Score Lead
nodes.notes: Scores leads 0-100 based on company size (40pts max) + industry (35pts max) + bonuses. Thresholds: 60+ = hot, 30-59 = warm, <30 = cold. Existing clients are flagged separately.

nodes.name: Is Hot Lead?
nodes.notes: First branch: separates hot leads for immediate calendar booking.

nodes.name: Is Warm Lead?
nodes.notes: Second branch: separates warm leads for brochure email.

nodes.name: Book Sales Calendar
nodes.notes: Mocks calendar booking for hot leads. Handles the WhatsApp edge case where no email exists — flags for manual follow-up instead of silently failing.

nodes.name: Send Brochure Email
nodes.notes: Mocks brochure email for warm leads. Email is personalised with company name and industry. Handles missing email edge case.

nodes.name: Add to Drip List
nodes.notes: Adds cold leads to a drip sequence. Industry-specific sequences for better relevance. First email scheduled 3 days out.

nodes.name: Flag Existing Client
nodes.notes: Existing clients are routed out of the new lead pipeline entirely. Flags for account management team to handle — could be an upsell opportunity.

nodes.name: Merge All Outcomes
nodes.notes: Reunites all paths (hot/warm/cold/existing) for the final logging step.

nodes.name: Log Outcome
nodes.notes: Central audit log for every lead processed. Flags manual follow-ups. In production this writes to a Google Sheet or database so the team has a full record.

nodes.name: Error Handler
nodes.notes: Catches failures from any node. Logs which node failed and why. In production this alerts the team so no lead is silently lost.
